text:
In this story:

In a preview of this summer芒聙聶s Olympic men芒聙聶s discus final in Paris, Cal junior Mykolas Alekna emerged with a decisive victory over his rivals, winning at the Wanda Diamond League meet at Marrakesh, Morocco on Sunday.脗

Alekna, who broke the event芒聙聶s 39-year-old world record on April 14 in Oklahoma, had the three best throws of the day in competition against脗聽 powerhouse throwers Daniel Stahl and Kristjan Ceh.

Redshirting at Cal this spring to prepare for the Paris Games, Alekna delivered the winning throw of 231 feet, 11 inches (70.70 meters) 芒聙聰 nearly 10 feet beyond what anyone else managed.

The 21-year-old Lithuanian's two next best throws 芒聙聰 229-5 (69.94) and 225-4 (68.68) 芒聙聰 also would have won the competition.

芒聙聹I feel good. I芒聙聶m happy with my result 芒聙聰 over 70 meters, that芒聙聶s what I was hoping for,芒聙聺 Alekna told World Athletics the day after flying 6,000 miles from the Bay Area to Morocco. 芒聙聹I wasn芒聙聶t as fresh but I芒聙聶m happy with it.芒聙聺

Alekna uncorked a world-record throw of 243-11 (74.35) last month to serve further notice that he will be a serious contender at the Olympics. He finished second at the World Championships in 2022 as a 19-year-old, then wound up third at the 芒聙聶23 Worlds.

This was his first meeting of the year against Stahl (the 31-year-old Swede who won the Tokyo Olympics and owns two World Championship titles)脗聽 and Ceh (a 25-year-old from Slovenia, who won the 2022 World title).

Australia芒聙聶s Matthew Denny, the fourth-place finisher at the Tokyo Olympics, took second place with a mark of 222-2 (67.74). Stahl wound up third at 221-5 (67.49) while Ceh fouled four times and placed seventh at 212-1 (64.64).

Asked what it means to beat Stahl and Ceh in their first clash of the season, Alekna said, 芒聙聹It gives a lot of confidence. I芒聙聶m happy I can compete with the best in the world. It was a great competition. Yeah, it gives me confidence.芒聙聺

He has three more Diamond League meets, starting May 30 at Oslo.

"Three more meets with all the best before the Olympics,芒聙聺 Alekna said. 芒聙聹I think it芒聙聶s a good way to prepare.芒聙聺

Martynas Alekna, Mykolas芒聙聶 older brother, finished ninth with a mark of 209-9 (63.93).

Jeff Faraudo was a sports writer for Bay Area daily newspapers since he was 17 years old, and was the Oakland Tribune's Cal beat writer for 24 years. He covered eight Final Fours, four NBA Finals and four Summer Olympics.脗

title: Cal Discus Star Mykolas Alekna Tops Likely Paris Olympic Rivals at Marrakesh
description: The 21-year-old Lithuanian beats Sweden's Daniel Stahl and Slovenia's Kristjan Ceh
